metadata.title: Free Email Signature Generator – HTML for Gmail & Outlook
metadata.description: Create a professional HTML email signature without coding. Add contact details, logo, social media links and copy to Gmail or Outlook. No signup, no limits.

hero.title: Create a professional HTML email signature
hero.description: Build a professional email signature in minutes. Enter your details, choose colors, and copy the ready HTML code into Gmail, Outlook, or any email client.

software.name: Free email signature generator online
software.description: Free HTML email signature generator. Add contact details, CTA link, and social media profiles, then copy the ready code into Gmail, Outlook, and other email clients.
featureList: Logo or profile picture with shape and size options
Social media icons (12 platforms: LinkedIn, Instagram, Facebook, TikTok, YouTube, X, GitHub, Dribbble, Behance, WhatsApp, Telegram, Pinterest)
Individual text style for each signature element
CTA button with custom text and link
Live signature preview with background switching (light, dark, checkerboard)
HTML source code preview in modal
Export and import configuration as JSON file
Compatible with Gmail, Outlook, Apple Mail

howTo.name: How to use the email signature generator
howTo.description: How to create a professional HTML email signature using the free Arteon generator.

steps.name: Choose a signature layout
steps.text: Select one of eight available layouts: Standard, Top bar, Labels left, Centered, Compact, Two columns, Minimalist, or Bottom bar.

steps.name: Fill in contact details
steps.text: In the Details tab, enter your name, title, company, email, phone, and website URL. Optionally add an avatar or logo - after entering the image URL, you can choose shape (round, rounded, square) and size (40, 56, or 72 px).

steps.name: Add CTA buttons
steps.text: In the Buttons tab, add text and URL for the main button. Optionally add a second outline-style button and choose button rounding.

steps.name: Add social media links
steps.text: In the Social Media tab, paste URLs to your profiles. Supported platforms: LinkedIn, Instagram, Facebook, TikTok, YouTube, X, GitHub, Dribbble, Behance, WhatsApp, Telegram, and Pinterest.

steps.name: Customize appearance
steps.text: In the Appearance tab, choose a color theme, font, and text size. In the Text Style tab, change colors and sizes of individual elements.

steps.name: Add disclaimer and configure separator
steps.text: In the Disclaimer / Legal tab, enter legal text and configure the separator line - choose style (solid, dashed, dotted), thickness (1–3 px), and color.

steps.name: Preview and copy signature
steps.text: The signature preview is available on light, dark, or checkerboard background. The Copy signature (Gmail / Outlook) button copies the ready code to clipboard. Also available: Copy HTML code, Download as HTML, View HTML code, and export configuration as JSON file.

contentBlocks.title: Why have a professional email signature?
contentBlocks.html:
An email signature (also called an email footer) is a block of information at the end of every sent message. It contains contact details, job title, company name, and often a link to the website or meeting calendar.

The recipient does not have to search for the phone number in previous messages - all details are readily available. A professional email signature also shows that you pay attention to details and maintain a consistent company image in business correspondence.

contentBlocks.title: How to use the email signature generator
contentBlocks.description: Creating a professional email signature takes just a few minutes:

items.title: 1. Enter details
items.description: Fill in your name, title, company, and contact details.

items.title: 2. Customize appearance
items.description: Choose a layout, colors, add a logo, and social media links.

items.title: 3. Copy the code
items.description: The Copy signature button copies the ready code to clipboard. Just paste it in Gmail, Outlook, or another email client.

contentBlocks.title: How to fill in signature details
contentBlocks.html:
The Details tab contains all the fields you can fill in. Only two are required: name and email address. The remaining fields are optional and will appear in the signature only when filled in.

Fields in the Details tab

  • Line above name - additional text displayed above the name. You can enter the company name, tagline, or slogan.
  • Avatar / logo (image URL) - URL to a profile photo or company logo. The image should be square (min. 120×120 px) and publicly accessible online. After pasting the URL, additional options appear: Avatar shape (round, rounded, or square) and Avatar size (small - 40 px, medium - 56 px, large - 72 px).
  • Full name - required field. Enter your full name.
  • Name tag - short text next to the name, e.g., pronouns or abbreviated title.
  • Job title - your role in the company, e.g., Sales Manager, Marketing Specialist.
  • Company name - the name of the organization you represent.
  • Additional line - a short description of what you do or what you offer.
  • Email - required field. Enter your business email address.
  • Phone - contact number. Any format, e.g., +1 555 000 000.
  • Website - full URL of your website, starting with https://.
  • Address - physical company address (street, city, postal code).
  • Formal details - tax ID, license number, or other details required in your industry.

contentBlocks.title: Signature layouts - which to choose?
contentBlocks.demoHtml:
Signature layout

StandardAccent barTop barLabels leftCentered

John Smith

Web Developer

Arteon Agency

john@example.com

+1 555 000 000
contentBlocks.html:
The generator offers eight ready-made layouts. Each presents the same information in a different way.

  • Standard - classic vertical layout, fits most use cases.
  • Top bar - a horizontal bar separates the signature from the content.
  • Labels left - two columns: labels and values.
  • Centered - formal appearance, content centered.
  • Compact - everything in one line, minimal height.
  • Two columns - details on the left, contact info on the right.
  • Minimalist - only the most important information.
  • Bottom bar - colored bar with contact at the bottom of the signature.

contentBlocks.title: How to add CTA buttons
contentBlocks.html:
A CTA button (call-to-action) is a clickable element that directs the recipient to a chosen page. It can be a link to a meeting calendar, contact form, or offer. The generator allows adding two CTA buttons.

Main button

  1. Open the Buttons tab in the editor.
  2. In the Main button section, enter the text the recipient will see, e.g., Schedule a free consultation.
  3. In the CTA link field, paste the full URL. The address must start with https://.

Secondary button

You can add a second CTA button in outline style (transparent background with border). Fill in the fields in the Secondary button section the same way as for the main button.

Button rounding

At the bottom of the Buttons tab you will find the Button rounding option. Three variants are available: None (sharp corners), Slight (slightly rounded), or Full (oval button).

If you leave both fields empty, the button will not appear in the signature. If you fill in only one field, the button will also not be visible - both text and link are needed.

contentBlocks.title: How to add social media links
contentBlocks.html:
In the Social Media tab you can add links to your profiles. The generator supports twelve platforms: LinkedIn, Instagram, Facebook, TikTok, YouTube, X (formerly Twitter), GitHub, Dribbble, Behance, WhatsApp, Telegram, and Pinterest.

How to do it

  1. Open the Social Media tab in the editor.
  2. Next to each platform you will find a URL field. Paste the full link to your profile, e.g., https://www.linkedin.com/in/yourprofile.
  3. Fill in only the fields you need. Empty fields will not appear in the signature.

Social media icons

At the bottom of the Social Media tab you will find the Show icons next to service names option. When enabled, links will display as colored SVG icons instead of just names.

  • Icon size - options: Small (16 px), Medium (20 px), or Large (24 px).
  • Icon color - Platform colors (default colors for each platform), Accent color (uniform accent color), or Text color.

contentBlocks.title: Appearance customization
contentBlocks.html:
In the Appearance tab you can customize colors, font, and other visual elements of the signature.

Color themes

At the top of the tab you will find five ready-made themes: Dark, Blue, Purple, Green, and Gray. Clicking a theme automatically sets the accent color and text color. This is the quickest way to achieve a consistent signature look.

Colors

  • Accent color - used in the side/top bar, CTA button, and links. You can choose any color from the palette.
  • Text color - color of all text in the signature (name, contact details, disclaimer).
  • Background color - background color of the entire signature. White by default, but you can change it.

Font and size

  • Font - options: Arial, Verdana, Tahoma, Trebuchet MS, and Georgia. All are email-safe and will display correctly for the recipient.
  • Text size - three options: Small (12 px), Standard (14 px), and Larger (16 px).

Additional settings

  • Signature border - you can add a border to the signature by selecting any combination of sides (left, right, top, bottom). Selecting all sides automatically sets a full border. The border uses the accent color.

contentBlocks.title: Text style - colors and sizes of individual elements
contentBlocks.html:
In the Text Style tab you can individually customize the color and font size for each text element in the signature.

Available elements

Only elements that currently have content in the signature are visible. For each element you can set:

  • Name - displayed in accent color by default.
  • Job title - role in the company.
  • Company - organization name.
  • Contact details - email, phone, website (labels).
  • Social media - profile links.
  • Legal disclaimer - legal text at the bottom of the signature.

How to change color

Next to each element you will find a row of color options. Click a color square to select it. You can also add a custom color by clicking the colored square with a plus - choose a color and click Save. Custom colors (up to 8) are shared between all elements.

The reset icon button restores the default color of the element.

How to change size

In the second row you will find a size control with - and + buttons. The value shows the offset relative to the base font size (set in the Appearance tab). Range: from -4 to +4 pixels.

contentBlocks.title: Spacing between elements
contentBlocks.html:
In the Spacing tab you can precisely control the distances between individual signature elements.

Inner signature padding

At the top of the tab you will find the inner padding option - the distance between the signature content and its edges. Three values are available: 8 px (small), 16 px (medium), and 24 px (large).

Spacing between elements

Below the padding you will find controls for individual signature elements. Each control has + and - buttons to increase or decrease the spacing. Only options for elements currently in the signature are visible.

  • After name - spacing below the name line.
  • After title / company - spacing below the title and company name line.
  • After additional line - spacing below the description (if filled in).
  • After contact details - spacing below email, phone, and website.
  • After social media - spacing below profile links.
  • After CTA button - spacing below the button (if enabled).
  • Before disclaimer - spacing above the legal disclaimer text.

If you clear a field (e.g., remove the phone number), the corresponding spacing option will automatically disappear from the tab.

contentBlocks.title: Legal disclaimer - when to add it
contentBlocks.html:
In the Disclaimer / Legal tab you can add legal text that will appear at the bottom of the signature in smaller font.

When to add a disclaimer

  • Business correspondence - confidentiality notice and request to delete in case of incorrect delivery.
  • Industry requirements - some industries (e.g., legal, medical, financial) require specific information in every message.
  • GDPR - information about personal data processing, if the company is a data controller.

By default, the field is filled with a sample confidentiality text. You can edit, delete, or replace it with your own disclaimer. If you leave the field empty, no disclaimer will appear in the signature.

Separator line

Below the text field you will find the Show divider between contact details and disclaimer option. Enable it to add a horizontal line between contact details and the legal disclaimer.

After enabling the line, additional customization options appear:

  • Line style - options: solid, dashed, or dotted.
  • Line thickness - three options: 1 px, 2 px, or 3 px.
  • Line color - default gray (#e5e7eb). You can choose any color from the palette or restore the default by clicking the Default button.

contentBlocks.title: Auto-save and resetting settings
contentBlocks.html:
The generator automatically saves all settings in browser memory (localStorage). After refreshing the page or reopening the tool, data, layout, colors, and all other options will be restored.

How does auto-save work?

Every change (text, color, layout, spacing) is immediately saved in browser memory. Saving happens automatically in the background - without clicking any buttons.

How to reset settings

Below the signature preview you will find a Reset appearance button. After clicking, a confirmation dialog will appear. Confirming the reset will restore all data and settings to default values. This operation cannot be undone.

Export and import configuration

Below the signature preview you will find two additional buttons: Export settings and Import settings. Export saves all data, colors, layout, spacing, and text styles to a JSON file. Import loads a previously saved configuration from a file.

This feature is especially useful when you want to transfer the signature configuration between computers, share settings with colleagues, or keep a backup of your settings before a reset.

contentBlocks.title: How to copy the signature to Gmail or Outlook
contentBlocks.description: When the signature is ready, it only takes a few steps to add it to your email client:

items.title: 1. Copy the code
items.description: The Copy signature (Gmail / Outlook) button copies the signature to clipboard as formatted HTML, ready to paste.

items.title: 2. Open signature settings
items.description: In Gmail: Settings → See all settings → Signature. In Outlook: File → Options → Mail → Signatures.

items.title: 3. Paste and save
items.description: In the signature field, use Ctrl+V (Windows) or Cmd+V (Mac). The signature will appear with all colors and formatting. Save changes.

contentBlocks.title: Signature preview and export options
contentBlocks.html:
The preview panel displays on the right side of the editor and automatically updates with every change. The preview is sticky, so it remains visible even while scrolling through editing options.

Switching preview background

Above the preview you will find three background change buttons: Light (default gray background), Dark (dark background, useful for checking readability on dark backgrounds), and Checkerboard (checkerboard pattern, lets you see transparency and signature edges).

Action buttons

Below the preview you will find several buttons:

  • Copy signature (Gmail / Outlook) - copies the signature to clipboard as formatted HTML. Paste it directly in signature settings.
  • Copy HTML code - copies the raw HTML code of the signature to clipboard. Useful when you want to paste code in an HTML editor or CMS.
  • Download as HTML - saves the signature code as an .html file to disk. Useful for archiving or sharing with a developer.
  • View HTML code - opens a modal with the full HTML source code of the signature. You can review the code and copy it directly from the modal.
  • Export settings - saves the full signature configuration (data, colors, layout, spacing, styles) to a JSON file.
  • Import settings - loads a configuration from a JSON file. All settings will be automatically applied.
  • Reset appearance - restores all data and settings to default values (after confirmation).

contentBlocks.title: Who is the email signature creator for?
contentBlocks.description: The email signature tool is useful for anyone who sends business emails:

items.title: Entrepreneurs and freelancers
items.description: A professional signature in every message without hiring a designer.

items.title: Company teams
items.description: Each employee creates their own signature in the same style. Consistent identity across the entire company.

items.title: Sales representatives
items.description: A CTA button with a link to a calendar or offer increases the chances of booking a meeting.

items.title: Lawyers, accountants, doctors
items.description: Ability to add a legal disclaimer, license number, tax ID, or other details required in the industry.

contentBlocks.title: What makes this HTML email signature generator special?

items.title: Eight signature layouts
items.description: Standard, top bar, labels left, centered, compact, two columns, minimalist, and bottom bar.

items.title: Auto-save in browser
items.description: Every change is automatically saved in browser memory. After refreshing the page, the signature remains available.

items.title: Export and import configuration
items.description: The full signature configuration can be exported as a JSON file and shared with colleagues.

items.title: 12 social media platforms
items.description: LinkedIn, Instagram, Facebook, TikTok, YouTube, X, GitHub, Dribbble, Behance, WhatsApp, Telegram, and Pinterest.

items.title: Advanced customization

items.title: Multiple export options

items.title: Live preview
items.description: Every change is instantly visible on light, dark, or checkerboard background.

contentBlocks.title: Frequently asked questions about the email signature generator

items.question: Does the signature work in Gmail, Outlook, and Apple Mail?
items.answer: Yes. Signatures generated by the generator use HTML tables that display correctly in all popular email clients: Gmail, Outlook (desktop and web), Apple Mail, Thunderbird. The layout adapts to different devices, including smartphones.

items.question: Can I add a company logo?
items.answer: Yes. In the Details tab, paste the URL of an image (logo or profile photo). The image should be square (min. 120×120 px) and publicly accessible online. After pasting the URL, options for shape (round, rounded, square) and size (40, 56, or 72 px) will appear.

items.question: How do I copy the signature to an email client?
items.answer: The Copy signature (Gmail / Outlook) button below the preview copies the signature to clipboard as formatted HTML. In Gmail or Outlook signature settings, just use Ctrl+V (Windows) or Cmd+V (Mac).

items.question: Can I use the generator to create signatures for an entire team?
items.answer: Yes. The generator does not require login or registration. Each person on the team can open the tool, fill in their details, and copy the ready signature. To maintain visual consistency, agree on shared settings: the same layout, color theme, and font. You can also export the configuration as a JSON file and share it with colleagues - they just need to import the file and change only their personal details.

items.question: Can I add custom social media icons?
items.answer: The generator offers built-in SVG icons for twelve platforms: LinkedIn, Instagram, Facebook, TikTok, YouTube, X, GitHub, Dribbble, Behance, WhatsApp, Telegram, and Pinterest. Enable the Show icons option in the Social Media tab. You can choose icon size and color (platform colors, accent color, or text color).

items.question: The copy button is inactive - why?
items.answer: To copy the signature, fill in at least two fields: name and email address. Check that both fields are filled in.

items.question: Avatar or logo is not displaying - what to do?
items.answer: The image must be publicly accessible online. The URL should point directly to an image file (ending in .jpg, .png, or .webp). Images from Google Drive or Dropbox require a special sharing link.

items.question: The signature looks different for the recipient - why?
items.answer: Different email clients (Gmail, Outlook, Apple Mail) may slightly differ in how they render HTML. The generator uses safe styles that work in most programs. If you see major differences, make sure you are pasting the signature using Ctrl+V, not Paste as plain text.

items.question: CTA button is not appearing - what to do?
items.answer: The button requires both fields: text and link. Check that in the Buttons tab both fields are filled in and the URL starts with https://.

items.question: How to export or import signature settings?
items.answer: Below the signature preview you will find Export settings and Import settings buttons. Export saves the full configuration (data, colors, layout, spacing, text styles) to a JSON file. Import loads the configuration from a file and automatically applies all settings.

items.question: How to view the source code of the generated signature?
items.answer: The View HTML code button below the signature preview opens a modal with the full HTML source code. You can review and copy the code directly from the modal. Alternatively, the Copy HTML code button copies the raw code to clipboard, and Download as HTML saves it as a file.

items.question: Can I change the avatar shape and size?
items.answer: Yes. After pasting the image URL in the Data tab, additional options appear: Avatar shape (round, rounded, square) and Avatar size (small - 40 px, medium - 56 px, large - 72 px). Settings are applied in all signature layouts.

items.question: How to change the separator line style?
items.answer: In the Disclaimer / Legal tab, enable the Show separator line option. Additional options will appear: line style (solid, dashed, dotted), thickness (1–3 px), and color (default gray or any color chosen from the palette).
